name: January
description: New Year's, Janus, MLK Day, the inauguration — the first month of the year, named for the two-faced god.

questions.q: How many days does January have?
Which Roman god is January named after?

source.notes: Janus had two faces — looking back at the year past and ahead at the year coming.

questions.q: What is January's birthstone?
In British tradition, what is January's birth flower?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced 'Carnation' distractor (also a defensible American-tradition January flower) with 'Iris'; specified British tradition so Snowdrop is unambiguously correct.

questions.q: Which two zodiac signs cover January?

source.notes: Capricorn runs through Jan 19; Aquarius begins Jan 20.

questions.q: What date is celebrated as New Year's Day?
On what day of the year is U.S. presidential inauguration held (since 1937)?

source.notes: Set by the 20th Amendment. Before 1937 it was March 4.

questions.q: On which day each January is Martin Luther King Jr. Day observed in the U.S.?
On what date in 1986 did the Space Shuttle Challenger explode 73 seconds after liftoff?

source.notes: Killed all seven crew members, including teacher-in-space Christa McAuliffe.

questions.q: On what date in 1973 did the U.S. Supreme Court rule on Roe v. Wade?

source.notes: Overturned in 2022 by Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization.

questions.q: On what January date is Auschwitz Liberation / International Holocaust Remembrance Day observed?

source.notes: Auschwitz was liberated by Soviet forces on January 27, 1945.

questions.q: Which Australian observance, celebrated on January 26, marks the 1788 arrival of the First Fleet?

source.notes: A subject of ongoing debate, with some referring to it as 'Invasion Day' or 'Survival Day' for Indigenous Australians.

questions.q: What is the Christian feast on January 6 that commemorates the visit of the Magi?

source.notes: Also called Three Kings' Day or Theophany.

questions.q: On what date does the Chinese (Lunar) New Year typically fall in the Gregorian calendar?

source.notes: Determined by the lunisolar calendar — between Jan 21 and Feb 20.

questions.q: Which annual professional football championship is played in late January or early February?

source.notes: Currently scheduled for the second Sunday in February.

questions.q: Who became U.S. president on January 20, 1961, with his famous 'Ask not what your country can do for you' inaugural address?
Which annual major US college football game is held in Pasadena, CA on January 1 (or 2)?

source.notes: Played at the Rose Bowl Stadium in Pasadena since 1923.

questions.q: Which two months were added to the original Roman calendar (alongside making January the first month)?

source.notes: Added by King Numa Pompilius around 700 BCE.

questions.q: What was the original first month of the Roman calendar before January was added?

source.notes: March (Martius), named for Mars, originally began the year.

questions.q: Which annual film festival is held in mid-to-late January in Park City, Utah?
